extension.valueMarkdown:
In Georgia, Harold established with a new PCP and a geriatric neurologist at Emory. His MMSE dropped from 22/30 (mild) to 17/30 (moderate) over 6 months. He was switched from donepezil to a donepezil-memantine combination. His diabetes and hypertension management continued with his new PCP. He had one ED visit for a mechanical fall (no fracture, CT head negative).

Margaret, as his authorized representative, uses a patient-facing app to access Harold's records across both the Florida and Georgia systems.

Four sites across two states (FL, GA). Tests Permission Ticket constraints on jurisdictions (FL vs GA), organizations (4 providers), periods (pre-move Florida records vs post-move Georgia records), and scopes (can filter to just cognitive assessments or just diabetes management).

resource.description: Documents goals-of-care discussion with patient and daughter/authorized representative. Patient expressed preference for comfort-focused care and stated he does not want intubation or mechanical ventilation. Authorized representative confirmed consistency with patient's long-standing wishes. Recommendation for formal legal documentation update in Georgia noted.

Comprehensive geriatric neurology evaluation note documenting detailed history, full neurological examination, neurocognitive testing results (MMSE 17/30, MoCA 13/30), assessment of Alzheimer's disease progression from mild to moderate stage, medication change to Namzaric, advance care planning discussion, and referrals for OT and brain MRI.

Follow-up neurology office visit note documenting 3-month reassessment of moderate-stage Alzheimer's disease on Namzaric. Includes review of brain MRI (04/02/2025) showing progressive hippocampal and cortical atrophy consistent with disease trajectory, MMSE score of 17/30 (stable from January 2025), medication tolerance assessment, caregiver-reported functional status, and plan to continue current therapy with next follow-up in July 2025.

note.text: Staging updated from mild to moderate based on MMSE 17/30, MoCA 13/30, and documented functional decline in multiple instrumental and basic ADLs. Five-point MMSE decline over 10 months, accelerated by bereavement and relocation.

note.text: Discontinued at this encounter. Patient progressed to moderate-stage Alzheimer's disease; switched to donepezil/memantine combination (Namzaric).

dosageInstruction.text: Take one capsule by mouth at bedtime daily
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take one capsule by mouth at bedtime daily

note.text: Memantine component added for moderate-stage Alzheimer's disease. May provide additional symptomatic benefit via NMDA receptor antagonism. Counseled caregiver on expected benefits and side effects.

code.text: Repeat brain MRI for comparison to 2022 baseline imaging

note.text: To be obtained prior to next follow-up visit in 3 months for comparison to 2022 imaging to assess interval change.

code.text: Referral for occupational therapy — home safety evaluation and adaptive strategies for ADL support

resource.conclusion: MMSE 17/30, MoCA 13/30. Cognitive profile consistent with moderate-stage Alzheimer's disease. Prominent deficits in delayed recall, orientation, and visuospatial/executive function with relative preservation of language fluency and motor function.

basedOn.display: Repeat brain MRI for comparison to 2022 baseline imaging

coding.display: MR Brain WO and W contrast IV

code.text: MRI Brain without and with contrast

conclusionCode.text: Progressive neurodegenerative changes consistent with Alzheimer's disease

resource.conclusion: 1. Progressive neurodegenerative changes consistent with Alzheimer's disease. Moderate bilateral hippocampal atrophy, progressed from mild on the April 2022 comparison study. 2. Mildly progressive generalized cortical atrophy, most prominent in the temporal and parietal lobes. 3. Chronic small-vessel ischemic white matter changes, Fazekas grade 1-2, mildly progressed. 4. No acute infarct, intracranial hemorrhage, mass lesion, or hydrocephalus.
